Additional Features

  • Building Collision — A buildings index is included, providing in-game collision geometry for all non-filtered buildings.
  • Neighborhood Labels — The map includes municipal and neighborhood labels derived from administrative boundary data.

Methodology

  • MX data:
    • Processed INEGI's DENUE, CE, and CPV data sets for the entire country.
    • Estimated business job counts by taking the geometric means of strata.
    • Aggregated businesses to the block level.
    • Estimated uncounted/informal jobs via the ENOE data set.
    • Extracted relevant points for the bounding box of this map, then applied a modified version of Colin's gravity model.
    • Added special demand via the Demand Adder.
  • US data:
    • subwaybuilder-US-demand-generator
  • Border data:
    • Assumes 50% of crossing happen from US side, and 50% happen from MX side. Values rounded down to ensure an equal number of pops for both. Commutes to/from the border point are separately done for MX and US sub-maps (where pops live/work at the border crossing and work/live somewhere in the city), then pops are paired up to form commutes from MX to US and vice versa. A 30 minute border crossing time penalty is applied to the commute.

Data Sources

  • INEGI Censos Económicos (CE) 2024
  • INEGI Directorio Estadístico Nacional de Unidades Económicas (DENUE)
  • INEGI Censo de Población y Vivienda (CPV) 2020
  • INEGI Encuesta Nacional de Ocupación y Empleo (ENOE)
  • US Census Bureau LEHD Origin-Destination Employment Statistics (LODES)
  • US Bureau of Transportation Statistics Border Crossing/Entry Data
